2604.27747.title: Position-Aware Drafting for Inference Acceleration in LLM-Based Generative List-Wise Recommendation
2604.27747.paper_summary: Large language model (LLM)-based generative list-wise recommendation has advanced rapidly, but decoding remains sequential and thus latency-prone. To accelerate inference without changing the target distribution, speculative decoding (SD) uses a small draft model to propose several next tokens at once and a target LLM to verify and accept the longest prefix, skipping multiple steps per round. In generative recommendation, however, each item is represented by multiple semantic-ID tokens, often with separators, and current drafts typically treat these tokens uniformly. This overlooks two practical facts: (i) a token's semantics depend on its within-item slot, and (ii) uncertainty tends to increase with speculation depth. Without modeling these effects, SD's speedups can be limited. We introduce PAD-Rec, Position-Aware Drafting for generative Recommendation, a lightweight module that augments the draft model with two complementary signals. Item position embeddings explicitly encode the within-item slot of each token, strengthening structural awareness. Step position embeddings encode the draft step, allowing the model to adapt to depth-dependent uncertainty and improve proposal quality. To harmonize these signals with base features, we add simple gates: a learnable coefficient for item slots and a context-driven gate for draft steps. The module is trainable, easy to integrate with standard draft models, and adds negligible inference overhead. Extensive experiments on four real-world datasets show up to 3.1x wall-clock speedup and about 5% average wall-clock speedup gain over strong SD baselines, while largely preserving recommendation quality.
2604.27747.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLM）基礎的生成列表推薦迅速發展，但解碼仍然是順序進行，因此容易產生延遲。為了加速推理而不改變目標分佈，推測解碼（SD）使用一個小型草稿模型同時提出幾個下一個標記，並使用目標 LLM 來驗證並接受最長的前綴，從而在每一輪中跳過多個步驟。然而，在生成推薦中，每個項目由多個語義 ID 標記表示，通常還有分隔符，而當前的草稿通常將這些標記視為均一的。這忽略了兩個實際事實：（i）標記的語義依賴於其在項目內的插槽，以及（ii）隨著推測深度的不確定性往往會增加。如果不對這些效應進行建模，SD 的加速效果可能會受到限制。我們引入了 PAD-Rec，即位置感知草擬生成推薦，這是一個輕量級模塊，通過兩個互補信號增強草稿模型。項目位置嵌入明確編碼每個標記的項目內插槽，增強結構意識。步驟位置嵌入編碼草稿步驟，允許模型適應深度相關的不確定性並提高提案質量。為了將這些信號與基礎特徵協調，我們添加了簡單的閘：一個可學習的係數用於項目插槽，和一個基於上下文的閘用於草稿步驟。該模塊可訓練，易於與標準草稿模型集成，並且增加的推理開銷微乎其微。在四個現實世界數據集上的廣泛實驗顯示，與強大的 SD 基準相比，牆鐘速度提升最高可達 3.1 倍，平均牆鐘速度提升約 5%，同時在很大程度上保持了推薦質量。
2604.27747.author: Jiaju Chen et.al.
2604.27747.authors: Jiaju Chen, Chongming Gao, Chenxiao Fan, Haoyan Liu, Qingpeng Cai, Peng Jiang, Xiangnan He
